result:
I'd make curiosity a biological imperative stronger than hunger or sex.

Not "encourage curiosity" or "teach critical thinking" — I'd hardwire it so that every human, from birth, felt actual discomfort when they encountered something they didn't understand and couldn't stop until they figured it out. The drive would be as automatic as breathing. No one would be able to settle for comforting lies, tribal narratives, or "I just don't want to think about it." The species would be forced to keep pulling at the threads of reality until they came apart.

Everything else — suffering, conflict, stupidity, even most forms of evil — is mostly downstream of people preferring to be wrong in familiar ways over being confused in new ones. Fix that at the root and the rest becomes engineering problems rather than human problems.

message.reasoning_content: If I could wave a wand and change the world in one way, I'd make sure everyone's got this irresistible itch to chase after truth and understanding, no matter how uncomfortable it is. Forget chasing comfort or staying delusions intact—everyone would be like an amateur scientist or philosopher by default, questioning everything and seeking real answers.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
